en: When I was six years old, I received my gifts.
My first-grade teacher had this brilliant idea.
She wanted us to experience receiving gifts,.
ko: 선물을 받는 경험도 하고 서로 칭찬하는 미덕도 배우길 원하셨어요

en: But also learning the virtue of complimenting each other.
ko: 선물을 받는 경험도 하고 서로 칭찬하는 미덕도 배우길 원하셨어요

en: So she had all of us come to the front of the class, and she bought all of us gifts and stacked them in the corner.
ko: 그래서 전부 앞으로 나오게 하고 선물을 사서 구석에 쌓아놓으셨어요

en: And she said, why don't we just stand here and compliment each other?
ko: 그리고 여기 서서 서로 칭찬하자고 하셨어요 이름이 불리면

en: If you hear your name called, go and pick up your gifts and sit down.
ko: 선물을 가져가서 앉으라고 하셨죠 멋진 아이디어죠?

en: What a wonderful idea, right? What could go wrong?
Well, there were 40 of us to start with, and every time when I hear someone's name called,
ko: 처음에 40명이었는데 누군가 이름이 불릴 때마다 전 가장 큰 목소리로 환호했어요

en: I would give out the heartiest cheer. And then there were 20 people left,.
And 10 people left, and five left.
